golden: SSRF vulnerability using open(url) without validation

golden: SSRF vulnerability using open(url) without validation
candidate: WHAT: Functional regression in Disqus import task ignoring user-provided options. golden: SSRF vulnerability using open(url) without validation

golden: SSRF vulnerability using open(url) without validation
candidate: WHAT: TopicRetriever executes PollFeed synchronously inside the perform_retrieve method. WHY: Triggers a complete RSS feed download and processing for every unique embed request, stalling Sidekiq workers and causing redundant workload when multiple new URLs are requested simultaneously. HOW: Enqueue PollFeed as a separate background job or only trigger it if a shared lock/throttle for the feed URL

golden: SSRF vulnerability using open(url) without validation
candidate: WHAT: PollFeed#poll_feed performs a database lookup for every item in the RSS feed via TopicEmbed.import. WHY: Triggers N database queries per feed poll, leading to O(N) database overhead that scales with feed size and polling frequency. HOW: Pre-fetch existing embed URLs in a single query before the loop and only process items that are missing or have changed SHA1 values.

golden: SSRF vulnerability using open(url) without validation
candidate: WHAT: open(url).read is used without a specified timeout in import_remote. WHY: Can block Sidekiq worker threads indefinitely if the remote server is slow or unresponsive, eventually exhausting the worker pool. HOW: Use a library like Excon or Net::HTTP with explicit connect and read timeouts.
